items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) Order 1998
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes in the Isle of Man restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 1173 of 12th June 1998 on:

items.title: The Isle of Wight Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1998
items.description: This Order transfers to the Isle of Wight Healthcare National Health Service Trust on 24th February 1998 trust property held by the Isle of Wight Health Authority. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, Accounts and Trust Allocations Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Trial of the Pyx Order 1998
The Education (Inspectors of Schools in Wales) (No. 2) Order 1998
The Housing Act 1996 (Commencement No. 12 and Transitional Provision) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings section 83 of the Housing Act 1996 (determination of reasonableness of service charge and right to challenge landlord’s choice of insurers) into force on 11th August 1998 to the extent that it does not already have effect (and subject to the transitional provision in article 3). Section 83 was brought into force by S.I. 1997/1851 (c. 75), subject to the savings in paragraph 1 of the Schedule to that instrument (cases where court proceedings or arbitration under way). The effect of this Order is to enable a court to refer matters which were the subject of court proceedings as at 1st September 1997 to a leasehold valuation tribunal.

items.title: The Sheffield City Airport Licensing (Liquor) Order 1998
The Lincoln District Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1998
items.description: This Order transfers to Lincoln District Healthcare National Health Service Trust on 24th February 1998 trust property held by Lincoln and Louth National Health Service Trust. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, Accounts and Trust Allocations Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Trade Marks (Fees) Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Trade Mark (Fees) Rules 1996 (S.I. 1996/1942) except for rule 5(2) of those rules.

items.title: The Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules revoke the Registered Design (Fees) (No. 2) Rules 1995.

items.title: The Patents (Fees) Rules 1998
The Gas Act 1986 (Exemption) Order 1998
items.description: Section 6A of the Gas Act 1986, as substituted by section 4 of the Gas Act 1995, provides for the granting by order of exemptions from the prohibition contained in section 5 of the 1986 Act from carrying on the conveyance of gas without a licence. This Order grants such an exemption to Interconnector (UK) Ltd in respect of the conveyance of gas through the UK-Belgian gas interconnector to a pipe-line system operated by a public gas transporter (article 3). The exemption is subject to conditions relating to the provision of information to the Secretary of State, the Director General of Gas Supply, the Health and Safety Executive and a public gas transporter to whose system gas is conveyed in pursuance of the exemption (articles 4 and 5).

items.title: The North and East Devon Health Authority (Transfers of Trust Property) Order 1998
items.description: This Order transfers on 24th February 1998 to the National Health Service trusts specified in the Schedule to the Order trust property held by the North and East Devon Health Authority. A copy of the Schedule of trust property transferred to each National Health Service trust can be obtained from the Department of Health, Accounts and Trust Allocations Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Southwark) Red Route (Prohibited Turns) Experimental Traffic Order 1998
The Merchant Shipping (Liability of Shipowners and Others) (Rate of Interest) Order 1998
The Cattle Database Regulations 1998
The Humber Bridge (Debts) Order 1998
The Hertfordshire (Coroners' Districts) (Amendment) Order 1998
items.description: The County of Hertfordshire was divided into five coroners' districts by the Hertfordshire (Coroners' Districts) Order 1974. Two of these, St. Albans and Watford, were amalgamated to form the St. Albans-Watford coroner’s district by the Hertfordshire (Coroners' Districts) (Amendment) Order 1974.

items.title: The A23 Trunk Road (Lambeth) Red Route (Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1998
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cotswold Hills) Designation (Amendment) Order 1998
The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) Order 1998 Partial Revocation Order 1998
The Restriction of Liberty Order (Scotland) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under sections 245A(8) and 245C(3) of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 as inserted by section 5 of the Crime and Punishment (Scotland) Act 1997, regulate aspects of the monitoring by electronic and radio devices the compliance of offenders with requirements of restriction of liberty orders.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Upper Thames Tributaries) Designation (Amendment) Order 1998
The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Insolvency) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1998
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) Regulations 1989 so that local authorities and development bodies in England are exempt from the competitive tendering requirements of Part III of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 in respect of certain functional work (as defined in that Act) which was being carried out by a contractor in relation to whom any of a number of specified procedures under insolvency legislation has been initiated.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988(Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Insolvency) (England and Wales) Order 1998
items.description: Under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (competition), work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by local authorities only if particular conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Motor Cycles (Protective Helmets) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations revoke and consolidate with amendments the Motor Cycles (Protective Helmets) Regulations 1980 and their amending instruments (the “1980 Regulations”). The 1980 Regulations prescribed certain types of helmet recommended as affording protection to persons on or in motor cycles from injury in the event of accident. The types of helmet were prescribed by reference to conformity with certain British Standards and by appropriate markings.

items.title: The A10 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route (Prohibited Turn) Traffic Order 1998
The A2 Trunk Road (Bexley) Red Route Traffic Order 1996 Variation Order 1998
The Land Registration (Scotland) Act 1979 (Commencement No.11) Order 1998
The Social Security (Guardian’s Allowances) Amendment Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Guardian’s Allowances) Regulations 1975 (S.I.1975/515) with respect to a child whose parents are divorced so that at the death of one parent, where there was no maintenance assessment under the Child Support Act 1991 (c. 48) in force in respect of the other parent and the child, entitlement to guardian’s allowance may arise.

items.title: The Houses in Multiple Occupation (Charges for Registration Schemes) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations enable local housing authorities to make charges for dealing with applications for first registration of houses in multiple occupation and applications for renewal of registrations where a registration scheme made under Part XI of the Housing Act 1985 as amended by the Housing Act 1996 contains control provisions preventing multiple occupation without registration.

items.title: The Houses in Multiple Occupation (Fees for Registration Schemes) (Amendment) Order 1998
Coventry Airport (Designation) (Detention and Sale of Aircraft) Order 1998
The Fossil Fuel Levy (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The Local Government Pension Scheme(Management and Investment of Funds) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations replace regulations L4 to L8 of the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/1019) which are now revoked.

items.title: The Working Time Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 93/104/EC concerning certain aspects of the organization of working time (O.J. No. L307, 13.12.93, p.18) and provisions concerning working time in Council Directive 94/33/EC on the protection of young people at work (O.J. No. L216, 20.8.94, p.12). The provisions in the latter Directive which are implemented relate only to adolescents (those aged between 15 and 18 who are over compulsory school age); provisions in that Directive relating to children were implemented by the Children (Protection at Work) Regulations 1998 (S.I.1998/276). Provisions implementing that Directive in relation to adolescents employed on ships are to be included in separate regulations to be made shortly after the date on which these Regulations are made, and adolescents employed on ships are accordingly excluded from the scope of these Regulations (regulation 26).

items.title: The Education (Individual Performance Information) (Identification of Individual Pupils) Regulations 1998
The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route Traffic Order 1998
The Building Act 1984 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1998
The Magistrates' Courts (Procedure) Act 1998 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 2, 3 and 4 of the Magistrates' Courts (Procedure) Act 1998 on 1st September 1998 and includes transitional provisions in connection with sections 2 and 3. Section 5 of that Act came into force on Royal Assent; section 1 is not yet in force.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Code of Safe Working Practices for Merchant Seamen) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which revoke and replace The Merchant Shipping (Code of Safe Working Practices) Regulations 1980 (S.I. 1980/686), require the owner (or other person assuming responsibility for ownership) of each UK ship, other than fishing vessels or pleasure vessels, to carry on board and make available to workers “The Code of Safe Working Practices for Merchant Seamen” (published in 1998 and obtainable from The Stationery Office) (“the Code”).

items.title: The Amusement Machine Licence Duty (Monetary Amounts) Order 1998
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st October 1998, increases–

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) Amendment Regulations 1998
items.description: Under the Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers’ Compensation) Act 1979 (“the Act”) lump sum payments may be made to certain persons disabled by a disease to which the Act applies, or to dependants of persons who were so disabled immediately before they died.

items.title: The Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1998
items.description: This Order substitutes a new article 3 in the Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993 to empower the trust to build a new hospital to replace the Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh and other hospitals.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment) (Restriction of Liberty Orders) 1998
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Criminal Procedure Rules by prescribing the form in which a restriction of liberty order, an application to review any such order, and a citation to appear before a court which proposes to vary or restrict, or to address a reported failure to comply with a requirement of, any such order, is to be made.

items.title: The Safety of Sports Grounds (Designation) Order 1998
The Occupational Pension Schemes (Validation of Rule Alterations) Regulations 1998 (revoked)
The International Monetary Fund (Increase in Subscription) Order 1998
items.description: This Order authorises an increase in the amount of the United Kingdom’s subscription to the International Monetary Fund in accordance with a proposal for an increase in the United Kingdom’s quota under section 3(a) of Article III of the Fund’s Articles of Agreement. (Under section 1 of Article III, the subscription of each member of the Fund is equal to its quota).

items.title: The West of Scotland Water Authority (Loch Lossit, Islay) (Amendment) Water Order 1998
The Private Water Supplies (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Private Water Supplies (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”). Regulation 3(b) amends the principal Regulations so that they do not apply to a private supply used for the mashing process or for washing plant during the distillation of spirits. Regulation 4 introduces new charging provisions for councils. Regulation 5 allows councils to arrange for persons other than “relevant persons” in terms of the principal Regulations to take samples, as well as analyse them, and requires councils to be satisfied that those taking and analysing samples are competent to do so. Regulations 6 and 7 provide for reduced sampling frequencies for certain supplies when the premises served by those supplies are used for less than six months in any year. Regulation 8 amends the principal Regulations to take account of local government reorganisation.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (Commencement) Order 1998
The Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (Sentence Review Commissioners) Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules make provision with respect to the proceedings of the Sentence Review Commissioners (“the Commissioner”) in relation to the release on licence and recall of prisoners under the Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”). In particular, the Rules prescribe the procedure to be followed in connection with the following proceedings under the 1998 Act: an application by a prisoner under section 3(1) for a declaration of eligibility for release; an application by the Secretary of State under section 8(1) for the revocation of such a declaration; and the consideration by Commissioners under section 9(3)(b) of the cases of recalled prisoners.

items.title: The Conditional Fee Agreements Order 1998
items.description: Section 58 of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 provides that a conditional fee agreement which relates to specified proceedings shall not be unenforceable by reason only of its being a conditional fee agreement. Section 58(1)(a) excludes agreements in respect of criminal proceedings from the scope of section 58 and specified family proceedings. This Order replaces the Conditional Fee Agreements Order 1995, which specified a limited number of categories of proceedings, and specifies instead all proceedings (within the scope of section 58) without any exceptions.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Cherwell District Council) Order 1998
The Foreign Satellite Service Proscription Order 1998
The Education (School Inspection) (Wales) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) Regulations 1993, as amended by the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1993, the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 and the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1997.

items.title: The Education (School Performance Information) (Wales) Regulations 1998
items.description: This version of the The Education (School Performance Information) (Wales) Regulations 1998 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Gifts for Relief in Poor Countries (Designation) Order 1998
The Personal Equity Plan (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Personal Equity Plan Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/469 as amended by S.I. 1990/678, 1991/733, 2774, 1992/623, 1993/756, 1995/1539, 3287, 1996/846, 1355, 1997/511, 1716 (“the principal Regulations”)) and come into force on 21st August 1998. The first main effect of the Regulations is that no further subscriptions may be made to existing personal equity plans, and no new plans may be set up, on or after 6th April 1999. The Regulations also contain amendments to the reporting requirements of plan managers which are consequential on the ending of subscriptions.

items.title: The Individual Savings Account Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the setting up of accounts (Individual Savings Accounts) by account managers, to which an individual may make subscriptions, and through which those subscriptions are invested. The Regulations specify the individuals who may invest, permitted investments, and maximum investment limits, and provide for accounts to be managed by the account managers. The Regulations also make provision for relief from tax in respect of accounts, withdrawal of relief, and modifications of income tax and capital gains tax legislation in relation to accounts.

items.title: The Individual Savings Account (Insurance Companies) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 6th April 1999, make provision in relation to the individual savings account business of insurance companies (“ISA business”).

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Overseas Life Assurance Business) (Compliance) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The Republic of Serbia (Prohibition on Investment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations provide that any breach of Article 1 of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1607/98 of 24th July 1998 (“the EC Regulation”) is to be a criminal offence. Article 1 prohibits the making of new investments in the Republic of Serbia except where authorisation has been granted in accordance with Article 2 of the EC Regulation. Regulation 3 of these Regulations provides that such authorisations are to be granted by or on behalf of the Treasury, and makes it an offence to provide false information in connection with a request for an authorisation.

items.title: The Education (Grants for Early Excellence Centres) (England) Regulations 1998
The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (Education Action Zones) (Modification) Regulations 1998
items.description: Section 10(1) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 contains a reference to “maintained schools”, an expression which is defined in section 20(7) by reference to new categories into which schools will be divided on and after “the appointed day”, also defined in section 20(7) of the Act. The appointed day is expected to be 1st September 1999 and these regulations modify the reference to “maintained schools” until the appointed day.

items.title: The Superannuation (Admission to Schedule 1 to the Superannuation Act 1972) (No. 2) Order 1998
The Broadcasting Act 1996 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings into force, as from 29th January 1998, section 41 of the Broadcasting Act 1996, so far as it is not yet in force, for the purposes of the notification by the independent national broadcasters of their intention to provide a service for broadcasting in digital form pursuant to section 41(2).

items.title: The Civil Courts (Amendment) Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Civil Courts Order 1983 so as to close the County Courts at Holywell and Rochdale.

items.title: The Special Immigration Appeals Commission (Procedure) Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules prescribe the practice and procedure to be followed on appeals and bail applications under the Special Immigration Appeals Commission Act 1997. They include provision for—

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (Specified Organisations) Order 1998
items.description: This Order specifies those organisations which the Secretary of State believes are concerned in terrorism connected with the affairs of Northern Ireland, or in promoting or encouraging it, and have not established or are not maintaining a complete and unequivocal cease-fire. The organisations mentioned in the Schedule are specified organisations for the purposes of the Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (c. 35), which makes provision about the release on licence of certain prisoners serving sentences of imprisonment in Northern Ireland. The effect of specifying an organisation is that a prisoner who is, or who would be likely to become, a supporter of such an organisation is ineligible for release under the Act or, if he has been released under the Act, is liable to be recalled to prison.

items.title: The Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1998
The Education (School Teachers’ Pay and Conditions) (No. 2) Order 1998
items.description: This Order, which is made under section 2(1) of the School Teachers’ Pay and Conditions Act 1991 (“the Act”) refers to a document entitled “School Teachers’ Pay and Conditions Document 1998” (“the Document”) published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office, which is obtainable from Government book shops and through booksellers.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Geography) (Wales) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for provisions specified in relation to the first, second and third key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Geography in the National Curriculum (Wales)” to have effect for the purpose of specifying attainment targets and programmes of study for geography in relation to schools in Wales. In the case of pupils in the first and second key stages, for the school years beginning in 1998 and 1999 those provisions apply with the amendments specified in the Schedule to the Order, but otherwise all the provisions apply.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Art) (Wales) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for provisions specified in relation to the first, second and third key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Art in the National Curriculum (Wales)” to have effect for the purpose of specifying attainment targets and programmes of study for art in relation to schools in Wales. In the case of pupils in the first and second key stages, for the school years beginning in 1998 and 1999 those provisions apply with the amendments specified in the Schedule to the Order, but otherwise all the provisions apply.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Physical Education) (Wales) Order 1998
The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in History) (Wales) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for provisions specified in relation to the first, second and third key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “History in the National Curriculum (Wales)” to have effect for the purposes of specifying attainment targets and programmes of study for history in relation to schools in Wales. In the case of pupils in the first and second key stages, for the school years beginning in 1998 and 1999 those provisions apply with the amendments specified in the Schedule to the Order, but otherwise all the provisions apply.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Music) (Wales) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for provisions specified in relation to the first, second and third key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Music in the National Curriculum (Wales)” to have effect for the purpose of specifying attainment targets and programmes of study for music in relation to schools in Wales. In the case of pupils in the first and second key stages, for the school years beginning in 1998 and 1999 those provisions apply with the amendments specified in the Schedule to the Order, but otherwise all the provisions apply.

The Income Tax (Employments) (Notional Payments) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Employments) (Notional Payments) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/1212) (“the principal Regulations”).

The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment) 1998
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment) 1998 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment) Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules 1981 so as to—

items.title: The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations provide for a winter fuel payment to be paid to persons who fall within the categories prescribed in regulation 2 on any day within the qualifying period (defined in regulation 1(2)) of amounts specified in regulation 3. A decision as to entitlement may be made on the basis of official records held by the Secretary of State but not so as to exclude other evidence on a review of that decision under the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (regulation 4).

items.title: The Animals and Animal Products (Import And Export) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with changes the Animal and Animal Products (Import and Export) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2428) as amended by the Animal and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1111).

items.title: The County Courts (Forms) (Amendment) Rules 1998
The Family Proceedings (Amendment) Rules 1998
The Protection from Harassment Act 1997 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1998
The Non-Contentious Probate (Amendment) Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules amend the Non-Contentious Probate Rules 1987 as follows—

items.title: The Parole Board (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules amend the Parole Board (Scotland) Rules 1993 and the Parole Board (Scotland) Rules 1995. The Rules make some minor drafting alterations and–

items.title: The Dartford-Thurrock Crossing Tolls Order 1998
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st September 1998, fixes the amounts of the tolls to be paid to use the Dartford-Thurrock Crossing.

items.title: The Dartford-Thurrock Crossing Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with minor drafting amendments the Dartford-Thurrock Crossing Regulations 1994 and regulations amending those Regulations between 1995 and 1997. In addition, the following changes of substance are made.

items.title: The Pre-Sentence Report Disclosure (Prescription of Prosecutors) Order 1998
The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Amendment Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations 1998 (the principal Regulations) to provide that the qualifying week for entitlement to a winter fuel payment for the winter of 1998 to 1999 shall be the week beginning 9th November 1998. They also amend the categories of persons who are entitled to a winter fuel payment and exclude certain persons in receipt of the mobility component of disability living allowance who are hospital in-patients from entitlement to such a payment.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Registration of Ships) (Tonnage Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations provide for the enforcement of Council Regulation (EEC) 2930/86 defining the characteristics of fishing vessels (O.J. L274 25.9.86 p.1), amended by Council Regulation (EC) 3259/94 (O.J. L339 29.12.94 p.11), by amending the Merchant Shipping (Registration of Ships) Regulations 1997.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Tonnage) (Fishing Vessels) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Tonnage) Regulations 1997 to require a measurement for tonnage of fishing vessels of between 15 or more metres in length overall and 24 metres in length, to be done in the same way as for fishing vessels of 24 metres or more in length, for the purposes of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2930/86 defining the characteristics of fishing vessels (O.J. No. L274, 25.9.1986, p.1) as amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. O.J. 3259/94 L339, 29.12.94, p.11).

items.title: The Deregulation (Exchangeable Driving Licences) Order 1998
items.description: This Order reduces the burden upon persons holding driving licences issued by countries or territories outside the European Economic Area and wishing to acquire a British licence.

items.title: The Local Government (Committees and Political Groups) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government (Committees and Political Groups) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”).

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (Hanger Lane, Ealing) Red Route (Prohibited Turns) Traffic Order 1998
The Local Government (Discretionary Payments and Injury Benefits) (Scotland) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with amendments, the provisions of the Local Government (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 1979, as amended, Part II of the Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy and Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 1984 and the Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy) (Scotland) Regulations 1994. They also revoke Parts K and L of the Local Government Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1987 dealing with injury allowances and gratuities. Parts V and VI of these Regulations replace them. As a result, these Regulations contain all the provisions relating to discretionary payments and payments of injury benefits by employers to persons engaged in local government employment other than teachers and those engaged in the police and fire services (excluding payments under the Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy or Premature Retirement on Reorganisation) (Scotland) Regulations 1995).

items.title: The Secure Training Order (Transitory Provisions) Order 1998
The Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, made under sections 408, 537 and 537A of the Education Act 1996, apply in relation to schools in England. They consolidate with amendments the Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1996, as amended, which are revoked.

items.title: The Frenchay Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Frenchay Healthcare National Health Service Trust to alter the purpose for which the trust has been established, and to amend its functions.

items.title: The Greater Manchester (Light Rapid Transit System) (Ashton-under-Lyne Extension) Order 1998
items.description: This Order authorises Greater Manchester Passenger Transport Executive to construct works and compulsorily to acquire land and rights in land for the purpose of extending the Metrolink light rapid transit system to run from Manchester Piccadilly Station to Ashton-under-Lyne.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 1998
items.description: Section 59 of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 requires a local authority to set aside part of a capital receipt (“the reserved part”) as provision to meet credit liabilities. Regulations 5 to 10 amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance) Regulations 1997 (the “1997 Regulations”) to provide that the reserved part in the case of capital receipts which are not derived from a disposal of housing shall, subject to specified exceptions, be nil per cent. (the “nil per cent. rule”). For this purpose, housing means any land, house or other building in respect of which sums fall to be credited or debited in accordance with section 74 (duty to keep Housing Revenue Account).

items.title: The Scottish Legal Aid Board (Employment of Solicitors to Provide Criminal Legal Assistance) Regulations 1998
The Firearms Rules 1998
items.description: These Rules prescribe the forms to be used in connection with the grant of certificates and permits for the purposes of the Firearms Acts 1968 to 1997 and the registration of firearms dealers.

items.title: The Education (Plans for Reducing Infant Class Sizes) (Wales) Regulations 1998
items.description: Section 2 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 came into force on 24th July 1998. Section 2(1) requires every local education authority to prepare a statement (referred to in these Regulations as “the plan”) setting out the arrangements which the authority propose to make for the purpose of securing that the limit on the size of infant classes imposed under section 1 of that Act is complied with in relation to schools maintained by that authority. The plan has to be submitted to the Secretary of State for his approval.

items.title: The Education (Infant Class Sizes) (Wales) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations impose, for the purposes of section 1 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998, a limit on class sizes for infant classes at schools in Wales.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (Amendment) Order 1998
The Foreign Package Holidays (Tour Operators and Travel Agents) Order 1998
items.description: Article 2 of this Order prohibits travel agents (and those tour operators in a group of companies which includes a travel agent when the tour operator sells directly to the public) from discriminating in the price charged for a foreign package holiday, or by imposing an additional charge, against a person who does not buy travel insurance in respect of that holiday from the agent or tour operator.

items.title: The Deregulation (Taxis and Private Hire Vehicles) Order 1998
The Education (Infant Class Sizes) (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provisions in connection with the imposition of a limit on the size on infant classes at maintained schools in England and Wales under section 1 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998. They apply in relation to the admission of children to such schools for education in infant classes in the 1999—2000 school year.

items.title: The Education Act 1996 (Infant Class Sizes) (Modification) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make modifications to the provisions of the Education Act 1996 in connection with the imposition of a limit on the size of infant classes at schools in England and Wales by Regulations made under section 1 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (which come into force on 1st September 1998). The modifications apply during the transitional period before the coming into force of the provisions of the 1998 Act concerning (a) the new school framework, and (b) the admission of pupils to schools in that framework (which is expected to be on 1st September 1999).

items.description: This Order establishes the New Addington Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone.

items.description: This Order establishes the Newham Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 4 to 6) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone.

items.description: This Order establishes the “Next Step” North East Lincolnshire Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone.

items.title: The Salford & Trafford Education Action Zone Order 1998
items.description: This Order establishes the Salford & Trafford Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone.

items.title: The N. Somerset Education Action Zone Order 1998
items.description: This Order establishes the N. Somerset Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone.

items.title: The Education Action Forum (Proceedings) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the meetings and proceedings of an Education Action Forum. Amongst other matters, they make provision for the appointment of a vice-chairman, frequency and notice of meetings, the quorum at meetings and cases where a person must withdraw from a meeting of a Forum.

items.title: The Education (Fees and Awards) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Fees and Awards) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations (which further amend the Education (Assisted Places) Regulations 1997) are made in exercise of the power conferred on the Secretary of State by section 3(2)(g) of the Education (Schools) Act 1997 which was added by section 130(1) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998.

items.title: The Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) Regulations 1998
items.description: Section 8 of the Rent Act 1977 and paragraph 8 of Schedule 1 to the Housing Act 1988 except from the definition of “protected tenancy” and “assured tenancy” in section 1 of the respective Acts a tenancy granted to a person who is pursuing, or intends to pursue, a course of study provided by a specified educational institution. The tenancy must be granted either by that institution or by another specified institution or body of persons. These Regulations specify the educational institutions and other bodies for the purposes of those provisions.

items.title: The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (Infant Class Sizes) (Modification) Regulations 1998
items.description: Section 2 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 requires every local education authority (LEA) to prepare a plan setting out the arrangements which they propose to make for the purpose of securing that the limit on infant class sizes imposed under section 1 is complied with at schools maintained by that authority. And section 3 of the 1998 Act requires the Secretary of State to make Regulations providing for the payment of grants to LEAs in respect of expenditure incurred by them for the purpose of securing that the limit is met at schools maintained by them.

items.title: The Education (Allocation of Grant-maintained and Grant-maintained Special Schools to New Categories) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, made under Schedule 2 to the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“the Act”), apply in respect of the allocation of grant-maintained and grant-maintained special schools to new categories of schools under that Act.

items.title: The Education (Prescribed Courses of Higher Education) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The Education (Plans for Reducing Infant Class Sizes) (England) Regulations 1998
items.description: Section 2 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 came into force on 24th July 1998. Section 2(1) requires every local education authority to prepare a statement (referred to in these Regulations as “the plan”) setting out the arrangements which the authority propose to make for the purpose of securing that the limit on the size of infant classes imposed under section 1 of that Act is complied with in relation to schools maintained by that authority. The plan has to be submitted to the Secretary of State for his approval.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1998 (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The Education (Infant Class Sizes) (England) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations impose, for the purposes of section 1 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998, a limit on class sizes for infant classes at schools in England.

items.title: The Animals (Scientific Procedures) Act 1986 (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) Amendment Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”) which make provision in connection with the membership and procedure of NHS trusts established under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Key Stage 3 Assessment Arrangements) (Wales) (Amendment) Order 1998
The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for English, Welsh, Mathematics and Science) (Key Stage 2) (Wales) (Amendment) Order 1998
The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Technology) (England) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for the attainment targets and programmes of study set out in the documents published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Design and Technology in the National Curriculum” and “Information Technology in the National Curriculum”(ISBN 0 11 270888 9 and ISBN 0 11 270887 0 respectively) to have effect for the purpose of specifying those elements of the National Curriculum for technology in relation to schools in England, subject to an amendment to the text of the former document, in relation to the first and second key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Physical Education) (England) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for the attainment targets and programmes of study set out in the documents published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Physical Education in the National Curriculum” (ISBN 0 11 270892 7) to have effect for the purpose of specifying those elements of the National Curriculum for physical education in relation to schools in England, subject to an amendment to the text of the document, in relation to the first and second key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in History) (England) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for the attainment targets and programmes of study set out in the documents published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “History in the National Curriculum (England)” (ISBN 0 11 270885 4) to have effect for the purpose of specifying those elements of the National Curriculum for history in relation to schools in England, subject to an amendment to the text of the document, in relation to the first and second key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Geography) (England) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for the attainment targets and programmes of study set out in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Geography in the National Curriculum (England)” (ISBN 0 11 270886 2) to have effect for the purpose of specifying those elements of the National Curriculum for geography in relation to schools in England, subject to an amendment to the text of the document, in relation to the first and second key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Art) (England) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for the attainment targets and programmes of study set out in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Art in the National Curriculum (England)” (ISBN 0 11 270 890 6) to have effect for the purpose of specifying those elements of the National Curriculum for art in relation to schools in England, subject to an amendment to the text of the document, in relation to the first and second key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Attainment Targets and Programmes of Study in Music) (England) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides for the attainment targets and programmes of study set out in the document published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office entitled “Music in the National Curriculum (England)” (ISBN 0 11 270891 9) to have effect for the purpose of specifying those elements of the National Curriculum for music in relation to schools in England, subject to an amendment to the text of the document, in relation to the first and second key stages of a pupil’s compulsory schooling.

items.title: The Harrow and Hillingdon Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Harrow and Hillingdon Healthcare National Health Service Trust.

The Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1998
The National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1996 by—

items.title: The Education (Student Support) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1998, are the first Regulations made under section 22 of the Teaching and Higher Education Act 1998. They provide for loans for maintenance for eligible students attending designated courses of higher education beginning on or after 1st September 1998. Means tested grants for maintenance and fees for such courses will be made by local education authorities under the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1998 until 1st September 1999, when it is intended that an expanded version of these Regulations will provide for grants for fees as well as loans for maintenance.

items.title: The Teaching and Higher Education Act 1998 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings into force the provisions of the Teaching and Higher Education Act 1998 specified in article 2 on the day after the Order is made. These provisions all relate to the repeal of the Education (Student Loans) Acts of 1990, 1996 and 1998.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1998
The Community Drivers' Hours and Recording Equipment (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The Potatoes Originating in Egypt Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Great Britain, implement Commission Decision 96/301/EC authorising member States temporarily to take additional measures against the dissemination of Pseudomonas solanacearum (Smith) Smith as regards Egypt (OJ No. L115, 9.5.96, p. 47) as amended by the Commission Decision of 28th January 1998 authorising member States temporarily to take emergency measures against the dissemination of Pseudomonas solanacearum (Smith) Smith as regards Egypt (OJ No. L25, 31.1.98, p. 101).

items.title: The Housing (Right To Acquire) (Discount) Order 1998
items.description: This Order specifies the amount of discount for the purposes of section 17 of the Housing Act 1996 (supplemental provisions on the right to acquire). Tenants of registered social landlords who have a right to acquire their homes under section 16 of the 1996 Act are able to do so with a discount from the purchase price. The amount of the discount varies according to the area in which the dwelling is situated.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (No. 2) Order 1998
The Road Traffic Act 1991 (Amendment of Schedule 3) (Scotland) Order 1998
The Removal and Disposal of Vehicles (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, extending only to Scotland, amend the Removal and Disposal of Vehicles Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Excise Duty Point (External and Internal Community Transit Procedure) Regulations 1998
The Parking Attendants (Wearing of Uniforms) (Edinburgh Parking Area) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations prescribe functions during the exercise of which, a parking attendant must wear a uniform.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Exceptions at Key Stage 4) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations provide for the National Curriculum to be disapplied for pupils at Key Stage 4 in maintained schools in England, to allow attendance on a work-related learning programme. Regulations 2–5 contain definitions; regulation 6 provides for the extent of the permitted disapplication of the National Curriculum; and regulations 7 to 12 prescribe conditions. The latter include preparatory interviews, curriculum plans and induction programmes (regulation 7), mentors (regulation 8), meetings (regulation 9), timetabling (regulation 10) and reports and information (regulations 11 and 12).

items.title: The Fertilisers (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend further the Fertilisers Regulations 1991 (“the principal Regulations”) and implement:

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1998
items.description: This Order prescribes units of production for the assessment of the productive capacity of agricultural land and sets out the amount which is to be regarded as the net annual income from each such unit for the year 12th September 1998 to 11th September 1999 inclusive. This Order supersedes the Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/1962).

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Scotland) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations are the first Regulations made under section 73(f) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) with respect to loans. They provide for loans for maintenance for eligible students attending designated courses of higher education.

items.title: The Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts and Cable Programmes) (Educational Recording Agency Limited) (Amendment) Order 1998
items.description: The licensing scheme operated by the Educational Recording Agency Limited was certified for the purposes of section 35 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, by the Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts and Cable Programmes) (Educational Recording Agency Limited) Order 1990 (as amended by amendment Orders in 1992, 1993, 1994 and 1996) made under section 143 of that Act.

items.title: The Chiropractors Act 1994 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provision) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings into force on 14th August 1998 section 1(1) of the Chiropractors Act 1994 for the purpose of establishing the General Chiropractic Council (“the Council”). The duty of the Council, under section 1(2) of the Act, to develop, promote and regulate the profession of chiropractic, is brought into force for the purpose of enabling the Council to prepare for the exercise of its functions under the Act.

The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings certain provisions of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 into force on 8th August and 1st September 1998. The provisions brought into force are provisions relating to Education Action Zones and enabling powers relating to the designation of grammar schools.

items.description: This Order varies the Sole, etc. (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1997 to prohibit, during the period beginning immediately before 28 February 1998 and ending immediately before 1st January 1999, fishing for sole, plaice and certain other species of fish in certain sea areas.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Radio Installations) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace, with amendments, the Merchant Shipping (Radio Installations) Regulations 1992.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Lancashire) Order 1998

The Brucellosis and Tuberculosis (England and Wales) Compensation (Amendment) Order 1998

The Sole (Specified Sea Area) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1998
items.description: This Order prohibits fishing for sole in sea area ICES Statistical Division VIIe by any British fishing boat which does not exceed 10 metres in length or which is used for the purposes of conveying persons wishing to fish for pleasure (article 2).

items.title: The Education (Funding for Teacher Training) Designation Order 1998
The Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Amendment (No. 3) Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Order 1997 (“the principal Order”) which specifies descriptions and classes of prescription only medicines (i.e. medicinal products which, subject to exemptions, may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by an appropriate practitioner and which may be administered only by or in accordance with the directions of such a practitioner).

items.title: The Housing (Change of Landlord) (Payment of Disposal Cost by Instalments) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1998
The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (Appointed Day) Order 1998
The Newcastle Education Action Zone (No. 2) Order 1998
items.description: This Order establishes the Newcastle Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 5 and 6) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone. The Order revokes the Newcastle Education Action Zone Order (S.I. 1998/1958) which contained a number of errors.

items.title: The Blackburn with Darwen Education Action Zone Order 1998
items.description: This Order establishes the Blackburn with Darwen Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in the Schedule. The Order also provides (in articles 5 and 6) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the Zone. The Order revokes and replaces the Blackburn and Darwen Education Action Zone Order 1998 (S.I. 1998 No. 1953), which contained a number of errors.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Compulsory Insurance: Ships Receiving Trans-shipped Fish) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make insurance, or another form of financial security, compulsory for any ship in respect of which a trans-shipment licence issued under section 4A of the Sea Fish (Conservation) Act 1967 is in force. These trans-shipment licences are issued to all ships involved in trans-shipment operations in respect of pelagic fish in United Kingdom waters, whether the ship is used to process fish or to store and refrigerate it.

items.title: The Meat (Hygiene and Inspection) (Charges) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations implement in Great Britain the provisions relating to charges for meat hygiene inspections of Council Directive 85/73/EEC on the financing of veterinary inspections and controls covered by Directives 89/662/EEC, 90/425/EEC, 90/675/EEC and 91/496/EEC of which an amended and consolidated text is annexed to Council Directive 96/43/EC (OJ No. L162, 1.7.96, p.1). In pursuance of Article 4 of Council Directive 85/73/EEC, they also provide for a charge to be levied in relation to hygiene inspection at slaughter of other land mammals and birds for which no standard charge is specified. The charges encompass any monitoring of the welfare of animals slaughtered for human consumption in slaughterhouses carried out under the Welfare of Animals (Slaughter or Killing) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/731).

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Act 1998 (Commencement) Order 1998
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 2, 3 and 6(2) of and the Schedule to the Education (Student Loans) Act 1998 on 1st March 1998. Sections 2 and 3 provide for the prescribing of the terms of student loans by regulation and for the administration of student loans respectively, and section 6(2) and the Schedule provide for minor consequential repeals.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st March 1998, supersede the Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1997, and the Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) Regulations 1997, which are revoked (regulation 2).

items.title: The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provision in consequence of the commencement of section 13 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998. Section 13 substitutes a new section 3 of the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991. The regulations modify paragraph 7 of Schedule 32 to the 1998 Act, which makes transitional provision arising from the amendment made by section 13. The effect of the transitional provision in these Regulations is as follows:–

items.title: The Mid Essex Hospital Services National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Mid Essex Hospital Services National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1991 (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Mid Essex Hospital Services National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (New Deal) (No.2) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend certain provisions in the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1973) and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2887), in relation to those persons who are undertaking qualifying courses as defined for the purposes of Parts II and IV of the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Transitional Provisions etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Local Government Pension Scheme (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1997 (“the Transitional Regulations”), the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) and the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”). The Transitional Regulations make provision for the transition from the arrangements for the Local Government Pension Scheme which are set out in the 1995 Regulations to those set out in the 1997 Regulations.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning) Order 1998 Partial Revocation (No.3) Order 1998
The Building Societies (Transfer of Business) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations replace the previous transfer regulations revoked by regulation 10. They prescribe certain matters connected with the transfer of the property, rights and liabilities of a building society to a company which may, by virtue of sections 97(3) and (12) of the Building Societies Act 1986 (“the Act”), be either a company specially formed by the society (and by no others than its nominees) for the purpose of assuming and conducting its business in its place or an existing company carrying on business as a going concern.

items.title: The Education (Funding for Teacher Training) Designation (No. 2) Order 1998

The Local Authorities (Alteration of Requisite Calculations) Regulations 1998
items.description: Sections 32 and 43 of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”) set out respectively how a billing authority and a major precepting authority are to calculate their budget requirements for a financial year and sections 33 and 44 of the 1992 Act set out respectively how a billing authority and a major precepting authority are to calculate the basic amount of their council tax.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Child Care and Maintenance Rules) (Amendment No.2) 1998
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends rule 3.59 (appeals) of the Act of Sederunt (Child Care and Maintenance Rules) 1997 by:

items.title: The River Esk Salmon Fishery District (Baits and Lures) Regulations 1998
The Nitrate Sensitive Areas (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations, which extend only to England and come into force on 30th September 1998, modify and amend the Nitrate Sensitive Areas Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/1729) (“the principal Regulations”) which allow the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (“the Minister”) to make payments of aid to farmers in nitrate sensitive areas who give undertakings to manage their land in accordance with a scheme as described therein.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (National Band Three Limited) Order 1998
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to National Band Three Limited a licence on 12 December 1997 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 3rd September 1998. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, National Band Three Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th October 1998.

items.title: The Local Government Changes for England (Council Tax) (Transitional Reduction) Regulations 1998
items.description: Under Part II of the Local Government Act 1992, where recommendations for changes to local government in England are made by the Local Government Commission, the Secretary of State may make an order (“a reorganisation order”). The Local Government Changes for England (Finance) Regulations 1994 make transitional provision for local authorities subject to a reorganisation order.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Tetralink Telecommunications Limited) Order 1998
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Tetralink Telecommunications Limited a licence on 12 December 1997 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 3rd September 1998. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Tetralink Telecommunications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th October 1998.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (TeleDanmark A/S) Order 1998
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to TeleDanmark A/S a licence on 27th April 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 3rd September 1998. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, TeleDanmark A/S will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th October 1998.

The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Amendment) Regulations 1998
The District of Purbeck (Electoral Changes) Order 1998
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Purbeck.

items.title: The Police (Secretary of State’s Objectives) Order 1998
The Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit Amendment (New Deal) Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations amend certain provisions in the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) and in the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814) in relation to those persons who are undertaking qualifying courses as defined for the purposes of Parts II and IV of the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207) in regulation 17A(7) of those Regulations.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Grants) Regulations 1998
The Children (Allocation of Proceedings) (Amendment) Order 1998
The Magistrates' Courts (Miscellaneous Amendments) Rules 1998
The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules 1998
The Investor Compensation Scheme Regulations 1998
items.description: These Regulations are part of the implementation of the Investor Compensation Schemes Directive (Directive 97/9/EC of 3 March 1997) (“the ICD”).

items.title: The New Deal (Miscellaneous Provisions) Order 1998
items.description: This Order provides that, for the purposes of Part I of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4) and the Jobseekers Act 1995 (c. 18) and of the subordinate legislations specified in the Schedule to the Order, a person using facilities provided in pursuance of any of the employment or employment-related training programmes known as “the Full-time Education and Training Option”, “the Voluntary Sector Option” and “the Environment Task Force Option”under the arrangements known as the New Deal and receiving or entitled to receive from the Secretary of State a training allowance in connection with the use of those facilities shall be treated as participating in arrangements for training under sections 2 of the Employment and Training Act 1973 (c. 50). Any payment made to such a person in connection with his use of those facilities shall be treated as a payment of training allowance made in respect of such training.

items.title: The Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (General Sale List) Amendment Order 1998
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (General Sale List) Order 1984 (“the principal Order”) which specifies classes of medicinal products which can with reasonable safety be sold or supplied otherwise than by or under the supervision of a pharmacist (the general sale list).

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route (Bus Lanes) Experimental Traffic Order 1998
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Avon Valley) Designation (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Avon Valley) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/84) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the Avon Valley as an environmentally sensitive area, in implementation of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35) as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (North Peak) Designation (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1998
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (North Peak) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/457) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the North Peak as an environmentally sensitive area, in implementation of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35) as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (South Wessex Downs) Designation (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1998

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Lake District) Designation (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1998
